attacking_style: Deploys leader with 2km or less remaining after extended domestique tempo control. Prefers penultimate or final climb attacks rather than long-range moves, relying on overwhelming power in final kilometers.
team_tactics: High-tempo chase work from distance (40-100km out) to control gaps and eliminate threats, followed by staged domestique deployment (Conrad→Majka pattern) to save leader until final 2km. Prioritizes bonus seconds and psychological positioning even without GC threat.
terrain_preference: HC climbs where domestique tempo can isolate rivals before leader attacks. Undulating terrain for pre-positioning attacks. Stronger on sustained climbs than defending in chaotic/weather conditions.
vulnerability: Fragile team cohesion under pressure (abandoned Almeida mid-race). Depth cracks early on first HC climbs of Grand Tours. Poor tactical coordination when multiple leaders present (Soler-Ayuso-Yates split). Struggles when forced to chase alone without payoff.
key_patterns: Chases breakaways from 40-100km out repeatedly, often solo, burning domestiques early. (7+ instances)
Prioritizes bonus seconds obsessively, contesting intermediate sprints even without GC pressure. (3 instances)
Abandons teammates requiring support rather than waiting, revealing cohesion fragility. (2+ instances)
Deploys staged domestique chain high on climbs, saving leader completely until sub-2km. (4 instances)
Shows team depth issues on first HC Grand Tour climbs, cracking earlier than rivals. (2 instances)
Uses psychological tactics: early TT benchmarks, attacks from numerical disadvantage, pre-positioning moves. (3 instances)
form_trajectory: neutral: Strong TT depth emerging (Ayuso, McNulty, Vine) but persistent tactical coordination problems and early Grand Tour vulnerability suggest systemic issues offsetting individual talent gains.
